name: The Trial of the Warden's Gate

meta.author: NoxDev sample
meta.summary: A single-check PORTABLE scenario (spec §2.5, P2). It names one canonical SKILL-TEST (attribute 'prowess', difficulty 'hard') and routes on canonical outcome bands (success / partial / failure) to three distinct endings. It carries NO ruleset ('*') and NO native attributes — its effects touch only the universal var/flag/item vocabulary — so the SAME file resolves under ff-2d6, srd-d20, pbta and any user system with a portability block. Under one system it is a 2d6 roll-under, under another a d20-vs-DC, under a third a 2d6+stat threshold — same story, different math.

passages.title: The Warden's Gate
passages.text: A slab of banded oak bars the stair, and across it a Warden's sigil that will only yield to a hand that proves itself. There is no key and no trick — only the doing of it.

choices.text: Set your shoulder to the Warden's Gate and force it.

passages.title: The Proving
passages.text: The sigil flares. Whatever system of the world judges you now, it judges the same deed: can you force the Warden's Gate?

passages.title: Through Clean
passages.text: The gate gives all at once and you are through, the Warden's hoard laid open before you.

passages.title: Through, Barely
passages.text: The gate grinds open a hand's breadth at a time and takes its toll of skin and breath — but you are through, and it cost you.

passages.title: Held Fast
passages.text: The Warden's sigil throws you back. The gate holds, and it keeps whatever it is guarding.
